title: What is CSS?
theory:
What is CSS?

CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets. It is the language used to style HTML elements — controlling colors, fonts, layouts, sizes, spacing, and much more.

If HTML is the skeleton of a webpage, then CSS is the skin, clothes, and makeup. It transforms plain HTML into a beautiful, well-designed page.

Three ways to add CSS

1. Inline: Using the style attribute directly on an element.
<p style="color: red;">Hello</p>

2. Internal: Using a <style> tag inside the <head>.

3. External: Linking a separate .css file — the recommended approach.

challenge.instruction: Style the paragraph to have the color 'tomato' and the heading to be centered.
challenge.startCode:
Skill Orbit

Learning CSS is fun!
